explanations.note: meanings are empirical — measured over layouts consistent with the auction under the engine's 2/1 style, not quoted from a rulebook

stem.text: Pass (E): limited — no action available or chosen.
Pass (W): limited — no action available or chosen.
3♦ (N): jump raise — 7-9 HCP, 5.0 ♦ on average (n=128, measured)
Pass (E): limited — no action available or chosen.

options.text: 5♦ — jump raise. Next call is usually Pass (100%); most common continuation: Pass Pass Pass (100%). A strong engine chooses this 17% of the time here. Where it leads: 5DS (100%). The panel would split: 5♦, Pass, 3♠ scored within the noise (gap +0.8 IMPs, ±0.9).

Pass — pass: shows 12-14 HCP, balanced 62% of the time (n=128, measured). Next call is usually Pass (100%); most common continuation: Pass (100%). A strong engine chooses this 12% of the time here. Where it leads: 3DS (100%). Scored -0.8 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.9); wins on 48% of layouts, pushes on 0%.

3♠ — new-suit. Next call is usually Pass (100%); most common continuation: Pass 3NT Pass (64%). A strong engine chooses this 14% of the time here. Where it leads: 3NN (64%), 4DS (36%). Scored -0.9 IMPs vs the top choice (±1.0); wins on 36% of layouts, pushes on 29%.

3NT — notrump: shows 18-19 HCP, balanced 83% of the time (n=128, measured). Next call is usually Pass (100%); most common continuation: Pass Pass Pass (100%). A strong engine chooses this 29% of the time here. Where it leads: 3NS (100%). Scored -1.1 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.9); wins on 21% of layouts, pushes on 50%.

4♦ — raise. Next call is usually Pass (100%); most common continuation: Pass Pass Pass (99%). A strong engine chooses this 8% of the time here. Where it leads: 4DS (99%), 5DS (1%). Scored -0.9 IMPs vs the top choice (±0.9); wins on 48% of layouts, pushes on 1%. Best on essentially no layout in this simulation — shown for completeness.
